title: Device level reversible potassium intercalation into bilayer graphene
type1_summary: No files appear to be cleaned, figure-ready source data or tabular replot-ready datasets.
type2_summary: All provided files are raw Igor binary instrument outputs (.ibw), including very large files, consistent with unprocessed experimental measurements.
classification_notes: The dataset is uniformly raw instrument data in Igor binary format. The paper information provided does not indicate any source-data/cleaned-data deposit, and none of the files show tabular structure or figure-ready organization.

file_classifications.paper_evidence: The paper provides no data availability statement or indication of source-data/figure-ready deposits; the study context is real-time device-level measurements, which is consistent with raw experimental outputs being archived.
file_classifications.file_evidence: Igor binary instrument file (.ibw), 213.6 MB, explicitly identified as a raw instrument file from Zenodo. The large size and proprietary binary format strongly indicate unprocessed measurement data rather than replot-ready tables.
file_classifications.reasoning: This file is best interpreted as raw instrument output from a scanning probe/Igor workflow. It is not directly replot-ready and would require specialized parsing and likely preprocessing. With no paper evidence suggesting cleaned source data, the conservative classification is Type 2.

paper_analysis.summary: The paper reports real-time device-level experiments on reversible potassium intercalation into bilayer graphene, measuring charge transfer, diffusion, and electronic property changes during insertion and extraction.
paper_analysis.processed_plot_details: The paper appears to report device-level experimental measurements on reversible potassium intercalation into bilayer graphene, including charge transfer, diffusion, and electronic property changes, but no accessible dataset location is indicated.
